functions.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaExceptionClass
documentation:
Map value to a proper subclass of {class}`cudaError`.

Raises:

: ValueError: If the error code is not valid.

functions.name: nvitop.api.libcudart._extract_cuda_errors_as_classes
documentation:
Generate a hierarchy of classes on top of {class}`cudaError` class.

Each CUDA Error gets a new {class}`cudaError` subclass. This way try-except blocks can filter
appropriate exceptions more easily.

{class}`cudaError` is a parent class. Each `cudaError*` gets it's own subclass.
e.g. {data}`cudaErrorInvalidValue` will be turned into {class}`cudaError_InvalidValue`.

functions.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.__cudaGetFunctionPointer
documentation:
Get the function pointer from the CUDA Runtime library.

Raises:
    cudaError_InitializationError:
        If cannot found the CUDA Runtime library.
    cudaError_SymbolNotFound:
        If cannot found the function pointer.

functions.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.__LoadCudaLibrary
documentation:
Load the library if it isn't loaded already.

Raises:
    cudaError_InitializationError:
        If cannot found the CUDA Runtime library.

functions.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaGetErrorName
documentation:
Get the string representation of an error code enum name.

Returns: str
    A string containing the name of an error code in the enum. If the error code is not
    recognized, "unrecognized error code" is returned.

Raises:
    cudaError_InitializationError:
        If cannot found the CUDA Runtime library.

functions.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cuGetErrorString
documentation:
Get the description string for an error code.

Returns: str
    The description string for an error code. If the error code is not recognized, "unrecognized
    error code" is returned.

Raises:
    cudaError_InitializationError:
        If cannot found the CUDA Runtime library.

functions.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaGetLastError
documentation:
Get the last error from a runtime call.

Returns: int

: The last error that has been produced by any of the runtime calls in the same instance of
  the CUDA Runtime library in the host thread and resets it to {data}`cudaSuccess`.

Raises:
: cudaError_InitializationError:

  : If cannot found the CUDA Runtime library.

  cudaError_InsufficientDriver:

  : If the installed NVIDIA CUDA driver is older than the CUDA Runtime library.

  cudaError_NoDevice:

  : If no CUDA-capable devices were detected by the installed CUDA driver.

functions.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaPeekAtLastError
documentation:
Get the last error from a runtime call.

Returns: int

: The last error that has been produced by any of the runtime calls in the same instance of
  the CUDA Runtime library in the host thread. This call does not reset the error to
  {data}`cudaSuccess` like {func}`cudaGetLastError`.

Raises:
: cudaError_InitializationError:

  : If cannot found the CUDA Runtime library.

  cudaError_InsufficientDriver:

  : If the installed NVIDIA CUDA driver is older than the CUDA Runtime library.

  cudaError_NoDevice:

  : If no CUDA-capable devices were detected by the installed CUDA driver.

functions.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaDriverGetVersion
documentation:
Get the latest CUDA version supported by driver.

Returns: str

: The latest version of CUDA supported by the driver of the form {data}`'<major>.<minor>'`.

Raises:
: cudaError_InitializationError:

  : If cannot found the CUDA Runtime library.

  cudaError_InsufficientDriver:

  : If the installed NVIDIA CUDA driver is older than the CUDA Runtime library.

  cudaError_NoDevice:

  : If no CUDA-capable devices were detected by the installed CUDA driver.

functions.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaRuntimeGetVersion
documentation:
Get the CUDA Runtime version.

Returns: str

: The version number of the current CUDA Runtime instance of the form {data}`'<major>.<minor>'`.

Raises:
: cudaError_InitializationError:

  : If cannot found the CUDA Runtime library.

  cudaError_InsufficientDriver:

  : If the installed NVIDIA CUDA driver is older than the CUDA Runtime library.

  cudaError_NoDevice:

  : If no CUDA-capable devices were detected by the installed CUDA driver.

functions.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaGetDeviceCount
documentation:
Get the number of compute-capable devices.

Returns: int
    The number of devices with compute capability greater or equal to 2.0 that are available for
    execution.

Raises:
    cudaError_InitializationError:
        If cannot found the CUDA Runtime library.
    cudaError_InsufficientDriver:
        If the installed NVIDIA CUDA driver is older than the CUDA Runtime library.
    cudaError_NoDevice:
        If no CUDA-capable devices were detected by the installed CUDA driver.

functions.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaDeviceGetByPCIBusId
documentation:
Get a handle to a compute device.

Args:
: pciBusId (str):

  : String in one of the following forms: `[domain]:[bus]:[device].[function]`,
    `[domain]:[bus]:[device]`, `[bus]:[device].[function]` where `domain`, `bus`,
    `device`, and `function` are all hexadecimal values.

Returns: int

: A device ordinal given a PCI bus ID string.

Raises:
: cudaError_InitializationError:

  : If cannot found the CUDA Runtime library.

  cudaError_InsufficientDriver:

  : If the installed NVIDIA CUDA driver is older than the CUDA Runtime library.

  cudaError_NoDevice:

  : If no CUDA-capable devices were detected by the installed CUDA driver.

  cudaError_InvalidValue:

  : If the value of {data}`pciBusId` is not a valid PCI bus identifier.

  cudaError_InvalidDevice:

  : If the device ordinal supplied by the user does not correspond to a valid CUDA device.

functions.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaDeviceGetPCIBusId
documentation:
Get a PCI Bus Id string for the device.

Returns: str

: An ASCII string identifying the device.

Raises:
: cudaError_InitializationError:

  : If cannot found the CUDA Runtime library.

  cudaError_InsufficientDriver:

  : If the installed NVIDIA CUDA driver is older than the CUDA Runtime library.

  cudaError_NoDevice:

  : If no CUDA-capable devices were detected by the installed CUDA driver.

  cudaError_InvalidValue:

  : If the value of {data}`device` is not a valid device ordinal.

  cudaError_InvalidDevice:

  : If the device ordinal supplied by the user does not correspond to a valid CUDA device.

functions.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.is_available
documentation: Test whether there are any CUDA visible devices.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaSuccess
documentation: The API call returned with no errors. In the case of query calls, this also means that the operation being queried is complete (see {func}`cudaEventQuery` and {func}`cudaStreamQuery`).

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidValue
documentation: This indicates that one or more of the parameters passed to the API call is not within an acceptable range of values.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorMemoryAllocation
documentation: The API call failed because it was unable to allocate enough memory to perform the requested operation.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInitializationError
documentation: The API call failed because the CUDA driver and runtime could not be initialized.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorCudartUnloading
documentation: This indicates that a CUDA Runtime API call cannot be executed because it is being called during process shut down, at a point in time after CUDA driver has been unloaded.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorProfilerDisabled
documentation: This indicates profiler is not initialized for this run. This can happen when the application is running with external profiling tools like visual profiler.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidConfiguration
documentation: This indicates that a kernel launch is requesting resources that can never be satisfied by the current device. Requesting more shared memory per block than the device supports will trigger this error, as will requesting too many threads or blocks. See cudaDeviceProp for more device limitations.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidPitchValue
documentation: This indicates that one or more of the pitch-related parameters passed to the API call is not within the acceptable range for pitch.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidSymbol
documentation: This indicates that the symbol name / identifier passed to the API call is not a valid name or identifier.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidTexture
documentation: This indicates that the texture passed to the API call is not a valid texture.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidTextureBinding
documentation: This indicates that the texture binding is not valid. This occurs if you call {func}`cudaGetTextureAlignmentOffset` with an unbound texture.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidChannelDescriptor
documentation: This indicates that the channel descriptor passed to the API call is not valid. This occurs if the format is not one of the formats specified by {data}`cudaChannelFormatKind`, or if one of the dimensions is invalid.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidMemcpyDirection
documentation: This indicates that the direction of the {func}`memcpy` passed to the API call is not one of the types specified by {data}`cudaMemcpyKind`.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidFilterSetting
documentation: This indicates that a non-float texture was being accessed with linear filtering. This is not supported by CUDA.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidNormSetting
documentation: This indicates that an attempt was made to read a non-float texture as a normalized float. This is not supported by CUDA.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorStubLibrary
documentation: This indicates that the CUDA driver that the application has loaded is a stub library. Applications that run with the stub rather than a real driver loaded will result in CUDA API returning this error.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInsufficientDriver
documentation: This indicates that the installed NVIDIA CUDA driver is older than the CUDA Runtime library. This is not a supported configuration. Users should install an updated NVIDIA display driver to allow the application to run.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorCallRequiresNewerDriver
documentation: This indicates that the API call requires a newer CUDA driver than the one currently installed. Users should install an updated NVIDIA CUDA driver to allow the API call to succeed.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidSurface
documentation: This indicates that the surface passed to the API call is not a valid surface.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorDuplicateVariableName
documentation: This indicates that multiple global or constant variables (across separate CUDA source files in the application) share the same string name.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorDuplicateTextureName
documentation: This indicates that multiple textures (across separate CUDA source files in the application) share the same string name.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorDuplicateSurfaceName
documentation: This indicates that multiple surfaces (across separate CUDA source files in the application) share the same string name.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorDevicesUnavailable
documentation: This indicates that all CUDA devices are busy or unavailable at the current time. Devices are often busy / unavailable due to use of {data}`cudaComputeModeProhibited`, {data}`cudaComputeModeExclusiveProcess`, or when long running CUDA kernels have filled up the GPU and are blocking new work from starting. They can also be unavailable due to memory constraints on a device that already has active CUDA work being performed.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorIncompatibleDriverContext
documentation: This indicates that the current context is not compatible with this the CUDA Runtime. This can only occur if you are using CUDA Runtime / Driver interoperability and have created an existing Driver context using the driver API. The Driver context may be incompatible either because the Driver context was created using an older version of the API, because the Runtime API call expects a primary driver context and the Driver context is not primary, or because the Driver context has been destroyed.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorMissingConfiguration
documentation: The device function being invoked (usually via {func}`cudaLaunchKernel`) was not previously configured via the {func}`cudaConfigureCall` function.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorLaunchMaxDepthExceeded
documentation: This error indicates that a device runtime grid launch did not occur because the depth of the child grid would exceed the maximum supported number of nested grid launches.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorLaunchFileScopedTex
documentation: This error indicates that a grid launch did not occur because the kernel uses file-scoped textures which are unsupported by the device runtime. Kernels launched via the device runtime only support textures created with the Texture Object API's.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorLaunchFileScopedSurf
documentation: This error indicates that a grid launch did not occur because the kernel uses file-scoped surfaces which are unsupported by the device runtime. Kernels launched via the device runtime only support surfaces created with the Surface Object API's.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorSyncDepthExceeded
documentation: This error indicates that a call to {func}`cudaDeviceSynchronize` made from the device runtime failed because the call was made at grid depth greater than than either the default (2 levels of grids) or user specified device limit {data}`cudaLimitDevRuntimeSyncDepth`. To be able to synchronize on launched grids at a greater depth successfully, the maximum nested depth at which {func}`cudaDeviceSynchronize` will be called must be specified with the {data}`cudaLimitDevRuntimeSyncDepth` limit to the {func}`cudaDeviceSetLimit` api before the host-side launch of a kernel using the device runtime. Keep in mind that additional levels of sync depth require the runtime to reserve large amounts of device memory that cannot be used for user allocations. Note that {func}`cudaDeviceSynchronize` made from device runtime is only supported on devices of compute capability < 9.0.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorLaunchPendingCountExceeded
documentation: This error indicates that a device runtime grid launch failed because the launch would exceed the limit {data}`cudaLimitDevRuntimePendingLaunchCount`. For this launch to proceed successfully, {func}`cudaDeviceSetLimit` must be called to set the {data}`cudaLimitDevRuntimePendingLaunchCount` to be higher than the upper bound of outstanding launches that can be issued to the device runtime. Keep in mind that raising the limit of pending device runtime launches will require the runtime to reserve device memory that cannot be used for user allocations.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidDeviceFunction
documentation: The requested device function does not exist or is not compiled for the proper device architecture.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorNoDevice
documentation: This indicates that no CUDA-capable devices were detected by the installed CUDA driver.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidDevice
documentation: This indicates that the device ordinal supplied by the user does not correspond to a valid CUDA device or that the action requested is invalid for the specified device.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorDeviceNotLicensed
documentation: This indicates that the device doesn't have a valid Grid License.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorSoftwareValidityNotEstablished
documentation: By default, the CUDA Runtime may perform a minimal set of self-tests, as well as CUDA driver tests, to establish the validity of both. Introduced in CUDA 11.2, this error return indicates that at least one of these tests has failed and the validity of either the runtime or the driver could not be established.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorStartupFailure
documentation: This indicates an internal startup failure in the CUDA Runtime.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidKernelImage
documentation: This indicates that the device kernel image is invalid.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorDeviceUninitialized
documentation: This most frequently indicates that there is no context bound to the current thread. This can also be returned if the context passed to an API call is not a valid handle (such as a context that has had :func`cuCtxDestroy` invoked on it). This can also be returned if a user mixes different API versions (i.e. 3010 context with 3020 API calls).

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorMapBufferObjectFailed
documentation: This indicates that the buffer object could not be mapped.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorUnmapBufferObjectFailed
documentation: This indicates that the buffer object could not be unmapped.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorArrayIsMapped
documentation: This indicates that the specified array is currently mapped and thus cannot be destroyed.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorAlreadyMapped
documentation: This indicates that the resource is already mapped.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorNoKernelImageForDevice
documentation: This indicates that there is no kernel image available that is suitable for the device. This can occur when a user specifies code generation options for a particular CUDA source file that do not include the corresponding device configuration.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorAlreadyAcquired
documentation: This indicates that a resource has already been acquired.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorNotMapped
documentation: This indicates that a resource is not mapped.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorNotMappedAsArray
documentation: This indicates that a mapped resource is not available for access as an array.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorNotMappedAsPointer
documentation: This indicates that a mapped resource is not available for access as a pointer.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorECCUncorrectable
documentation: This indicates that an uncorrectable ECC error was detected during execution.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorUnsupportedLimit
documentation: This indicates that the {class}`cudaLimit` passed to the API call is not supported by the active device.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorDeviceAlreadyInUse
documentation: This indicates that a call tried to access an exclusive-thread device that is already in use by a different thread.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorPeerAccessUnsupported
documentation: This error indicates that P2P access is not supported across the given devices.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidPtx
documentation: A PTX compilation failed. The runtime may fall back to compiling PTX if an application does not contain a suitable binary for the current device.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidGraphicsContext
documentation: This indicates an error with the OpenGL or DirectX context.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorNvlinkUncorrectable
documentation: This indicates that an uncorrectable NVLink error was detected during the execution.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorJitCompilerNotFound
documentation: This indicates that the PTX JIT compiler library was not found. The JIT Compiler library is used for PTX compilation. The runtime may fall back to compiling PTX if an application does not contain a suitable binary for the current device.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorUnsupportedPtxVersion
documentation: This indicates that the provided PTX was compiled with an unsupported toolchain. The most common reason for this, is the PTX was generated by a compiler newer than what is supported by the CUDA driver and PTX JIT compiler.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorJitCompilationDisabled
documentation: This indicates that the JIT compilation was disabled. The JIT compilation compiles PTX. The runtime may fall back to compiling PTX if an application does not contain a suitable binary for the current device.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorUnsupportedExecAffinity
documentation: This indicates that the provided execution affinity is not supported by the device.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidSource
documentation: This indicates that the device kernel source is invalid.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorFileNotFound
documentation: This indicates that the file specified was not found.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorSharedObjectSymbolNotFound
documentation: This indicates that a link to a shared object failed to resolve.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorSharedObjectInitFailed
documentation: This indicates that initialization of a shared object failed.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorOperatingSystem
documentation: This error indicates that an OS call failed.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidResourceHandle
documentation: This indicates that a resource handle passed to the API call was not valid. Resource handles are opaque types like {data}`cudaStream_t` and {data}`cudaEvent_t`.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorIllegalState
documentation: This indicates that a resource required by the API call is not in a valid state to perform the requested operation.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorSymbolNotFound
documentation: This indicates that a named symbol was not found. Examples of symbols are global / constant variable names, driver function names, texture names, and surface names.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorNotReady
documentation: This indicates that asynchronous operations issued previously have not completed yet. This result is not actually an error, but must be indicated differently than {data}`cudaSuccess` (which indicates completion). Calls that may return this value include {func}`cudaEventQuery` and {func}`cudaStreamQuery`.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorIllegalAddress
documentation: The device encountered a load or store instruction on an invalid memory address. This leaves the process in an inconsistent state and any further CUDA work will return the same error. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorLaunchOutOfResources
documentation: This indicates that a launch did not occur because it did not have appropriate resources. Although this error is similar to {data}`cudaErrorInvalidConfiguration`, this error usually indicates that the user has attempted to pass too many arguments to the device kernel, or the kernel launch specifies too many threads for the kernel's register count.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorLaunchTimeout
documentation: This indicates that the device kernel took too long to execute. This can only occur if timeouts are enabled - see the device property kernelExecTimeoutEnabled for more information. This leaves the process in an inconsistent state and any further CUDA work will return the same error. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorLaunchIncompatibleTexturing
documentation: This error indicates a kernel launch that uses an incompatible texturing mode.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorPeerAccessAlreadyEnabled
documentation: This error indicates that a call to {func}`cudaDeviceEnablePeerAccess` is trying to re-enable peer addressing on from a context which has already had peer addressing enabled.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorPeerAccessNotEnabled
documentation: This error indicates that {func}`cudaDeviceDisablePeerAccess` is trying to disable peer addressing which has not been enabled yet via {func}`cudaDeviceEnablePeerAccess`.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorSetOnActiveProcess
documentation: This indicates that the user has called {func}`cudaSetValidDevices`, {func}`cudaSetDeviceFlags`, {func}`cudaD3D9SetDirect3DDevice`, {func}`cudaD3D10SetDirect3DDevice`, {func}`cudaD3D11SetDirect3DDevice`, or {func}`cudaVDPAUSetVDPAUDevice` after initializing the CUDA Runtime by calling non-device management operations (allocating memory and launching kernels are examples of non-device management operations). This error can also be returned if using runtime / driver interoperability and there is an existing {class}`CUcontext` active on the host thread.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorContextIsDestroyed
documentation: This error indicates that the context current to the calling thread has been destroyed using cuCtxDestroy, or is a primary context which has not yet been initialized.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorAssert
documentation: An assert triggered in device code during kernel execution. The device cannot be used again. All existing allocations are invalid. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorTooManyPeers
documentation: This error indicates that the hardware resources required to enable peer access have been exhausted for one or more of the devices passed to {func}`cudaEnablePeerAccess`.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorHostMemoryAlreadyRegistered
documentation: This error indicates that the memory range passed to {func}`cudaHostRegister` has already been registered.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorHostMemoryNotRegistered
documentation: This error indicates that the pointer passed to {func}`cudaHostUnregister` does not correspond to any currently registered memory region.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorHardwareStackError
documentation: Device encountered an error in the call stack during kernel execution, possibly due to stack corruption or exceeding the stack size limit. This leaves the process in an inconsistent state and any further CUDA work will return the same error. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorIllegalInstruction
documentation: The device encountered an illegal instruction during kernel execution This leaves the process in an inconsistent state and any further CUDA work will return the same error. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorMisalignedAddress
documentation: The device encountered a load or store instruction on a memory address which is not aligned. This leaves the process in an inconsistent state and any further CUDA work will return the same error. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidAddressSpace
documentation: While executing a kernel, the device encountered an instruction which can only operate on memory locations in certain address spaces (global, shared, or local), but was supplied a memory address not belonging to an allowed address space. This leaves the process in an inconsistent state and any further CUDA work will return the same error. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidPc
documentation: The device encountered an invalid program counter. This leaves the process in an inconsistent state and any further CUDA work will return the same error. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorLaunchFailure
documentation: An exception occurred on the device while executing a kernel. Common causes include dereferencing an invalid device pointer and accessing out of bounds shared memory. Less common cases can be system specific - more information about these cases can be found in the system specific user guide. This leaves the process in an inconsistent state and any further CUDA work will return the same error. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorCooperativeLaunchTooLarge
documentation: This error indicates that the number of blocks launched per grid for a kernel that was launched via either {func}`cudaLaunchCooperativeKernel` or {func}`cudaLaunchCooperativeKernelMultiDevice` exceeds the maximum number of blocks as allowed by {func}`cudaOccupancyMaxActiveBlocksPerMultiprocessor` or {func}`cudaOccupancyMaxActiveBlocksPerMultiprocessorWithFlags` times the number of multiprocessors as specified by the device attribute {func}`cudaDevAttrMultiProcessorCount`.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorNotPermitted
documentation: This error indicates the attempted operation is not permitted.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorNotSupported
documentation: This error indicates the attempted operation is not supported on the current system or device.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorSystemNotReady
documentation: This error indicates that the system is not yet ready to start any CUDA work. To continue using CUDA, verify the system configuration is in a valid state and all required driver daemons are actively running. More information about this error can be found in the system specific user guide.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorSystemDriverMismatch
documentation: This error indicates that there is a mismatch between the versions of the display driver and the CUDA driver. Refer to the compatibility documentation for supported versions.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorCompatNotSupportedOnDevice
documentation: This error indicates that the system was upgraded to run with forward compatibility but the visible hardware detected by CUDA does not support this configuration. Refer to the compatibility documentation for the supported hardware matrix or ensure that only supported hardware is visible during initialization via the `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES` environment variable.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorMpsConnectionFailed
documentation: This error indicates that the MPS client failed to connect to the MPS control daemon or the MPS server.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorMpsRpcFailure
documentation: This error indicates that the remote procedural call between the MPS server and the MPS client failed.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorMpsServerNotReady
documentation: This error indicates that the MPS server is not ready to accept new MPS client requests. This error can be returned when the MPS server is in the process of recovering from a fatal failure.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorMpsMaxClientsReached
documentation: This error indicates that the hardware resources required to create MPS client have been exhausted.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorMpsMaxConnectionsReached
documentation: This error indicates the the hardware resources required to device connections have been exhausted.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorMpsClientTerminated
documentation: This error indicates that the MPS client has been terminated by the server. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorCdpNotSupported
documentation: This error indicates, that the program is using CUDA Dynamic Parallelism, but the current configuration, like MPS, does not support it.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorCdpVersionMismatch
documentation: This error indicates, that the program contains an unsupported interaction between different versions of CUDA Dynamic Parallelism.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorStreamCaptureUnsupported
documentation: The operation is not permitted when the stream is capturing.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorStreamCaptureInvalidated
documentation: The current capture sequence on the stream has been invalidated due to a previous error.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorStreamCaptureMerge
documentation: The operation would have resulted in a merge of two independent capture sequences.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorStreamCaptureUnmatched
documentation: The capture was not initiated in this stream.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorStreamCaptureUnjoined
documentation: The capture sequence contains a fork that was not joined to the primary stream.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorStreamCaptureIsolation
documentation: A dependency would have been created which crosses the capture sequence boundary. Only implicit in-stream ordering dependencies are allowed to cross the boundary.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorStreamCaptureImplicit
documentation: The operation would have resulted in a disallowed implicit dependency on a current capture sequence from {data}`cudaStreamLegacy`.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorCapturedEvent
documentation: The operation is not permitted on an event which was last recorded in a capturing stream.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorStreamCaptureWrongThread
documentation: A stream capture sequence not initiated with the {data}`cudaStreamCaptureModeRelaxed` argument to {func}`cudaStreamBeginCapture` was passed to {func}`cudaStreamEndCapture` in a different thread.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorTimeout
documentation: This indicates that the wait operation has timed out.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorGraphExecUpdateFailure
documentation: This error indicates that the graph update was not performed because it included changes which violated constraints specific to instantiated graph update.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorExternalDevice
documentation: This indicates that an async error has occurred in a device outside of CUDA. If CUDA was waiting for an external device's signal before consuming shared data, the external device signaled an error indicating that the data is not valid for consumption. This leaves the process in an inconsistent state and any further CUDA work will return the same error. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorInvalidClusterSize
documentation: This indicates that a kernel launch error has occurred due to cluster misconfiguration.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcudart.cudaErrorUnknown
documentation: This indicates that an unknown internal error has occurred.

functions.name: nvitop.api.utils.set_color
documentation: Force enable text coloring.

functions.name: nvitop.api.utils.colored
documentation:
Colorize text with ANSI color escape codes.

Available text colors:
    red, green, yellow, blue, magenta, cyan, white.

Available text highlights:
    on_red, on_green, on_yellow, on_blue, on_magenta, on_cyan, on_white.

Available attributes:
    bold, dark, underline, blink, reverse, concealed.

Examples:
    >>> colored('Hello, World!', 'red', 'on_grey', ['blue', 'blink'])
    >>> colored('Hello, World!', 'green')

functions.name: nvitop.api.utils.human2bytes
documentation:
Convert a human readable size string (*case insensitive*) to bytes.

Raises:
    ValueError:
        If cannot convert the given size string.

Examples:
    >>> human2bytes('500B')
    500
    >>> human2bytes('10k')
    10000
    >>> human2bytes('10ki')
    10240
    >>> human2bytes('1M')
    1000000
    >>> human2bytes('1MiB')
    1048576
    >>> human2bytes('1.5GiB')
    1610612736

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__bool__
documentation:
Convert {const}`NA` to {class}`bool` and return {data}`False`.

```pycon
>>> bool(NA)
False
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__int__
documentation:

```pycon
>>> int(NA)
0
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__float__
documentation:
Convert {const}`NA` to {class}`float` and return {data}`math.nan`.

```pycon
>>> float(NA)
nan
>>> float(NA) is math.nan
True
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__add__
documentation:
Return {data}`math.nan` if the operand is a number or uses string concatenation if the operand is a string (`NA + other`).

A special case is when the operand is {const}`nvitop.NA` itself, the result is
{data}`math.nan` instead of {const}`'N/AN/A'`.

```pycon
>>> NA + ' str'
'N/A str'
>>> NA + NA
nan
>>> NA + 1
nan
>>> NA + 1.0
nan
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__radd__
documentation:
Return {data}`math.nan` if the operand is a number or uses string concatenation if the operand is a string (`other + NA`).

```pycon
>>> 'str' + NA
'strN/A'
>>> 1 + NA
nan
>>> 1.0 + NA
nan
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__sub__
documentation:
Return {data}`math.nan` if the operand is a number (`NA - other`).

```pycon
>>> NA - 'str'
TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for -: 'NaType' and 'str'
>>> NA - NA
'N/AN/A'
>>> NA + 1
nan
>>> NA + 1.0
nan
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__rsub__
documentation:
Return {data}`math.nan` if the operand is a number (`other - NA`).

```pycon
>>> 'str' - NA
TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for -: 'str' and 'NaType'
>>> 1 - NA
nan
>>> 1.0 - NA
nan
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__mul__
documentation:
Return {data}`math.nan` if the operand is a number (`NA * other`).

A special case is when the operand is {const}`nvitop.NA` itself, the result is also {data}`math.nan`.

```pycon
>>> NA * 1024
nan
>>> NA * 1024.0
nan
>>> NA * NA
nan
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__rmul__
documentation:
Return {data}`math.nan` if the operand is a number (`other * NA`).

```pycon
>>> 1024 * NA
nan
>>> 1024.0 * NA
nan
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__truediv__
documentation:
Return {data}`math.nan` if the operand is a number (`NA / other`).

```pycon
>>> NA / 1024
nan
>>> NA / 1024.0
nan
>>> NA / 0
ZeroDivisionError: float division by zero
>>> NA / 0.0
ZeroDivisionError: float division by zero
>>> NA / NA
nan
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__rtruediv__
documentation:
Return {data}`math.nan` if the operand is a number (`other / NA`).

```pycon
>>> 1024 / NA
nan
>>> 1024.0 / NA
nan
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__floordiv__
documentation:
Return {data}`math.nan` if the operand is a number (`NA // other`).

```pycon
>>> NA // 1024
nan
>>> NA // 1024.0
nan
>>> NA / 0
ZeroDivisionError: float division by zero
>>> NA / 0.0
ZeroDivisionError: float division by zero
>>> NA // NA
nan
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__rfloordiv__
documentation:
Return {data}`math.nan` if the operand is a number (`other // NA`).

```pycon
>>> 1024 // NA
nan
>>> 1024.0 // NA
nan
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__mod__
documentation:
Return {data}`math.nan` if the operand is a number (`NA % other`).

```pycon
>>> NA % 1024
nan
>>> NA % 1024.0
nan
>>> NA % 0
ZeroDivisionError: float modulo
>>> NA % 0.0
ZeroDivisionError: float modulo
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__rmod__
documentation:
Return {data}`math.nan` if the operand is a number (`other % NA`).

```pycon
>>> 1024 % NA
nan
>>> 1024.0 % NA
nan
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__divmod__
documentation:
The pair `(NA // other, NA % other)` (`divmod(NA, other)`).

```pycon
>>> divmod(NA, 1024)
(nan, nan)
>>> divmod(NA, 1024.0)
(nan, nan)
>>> divmod(NA, 0)
ZeroDivisionError: float floor division by zero
>>> divmod(NA, 0.0)
ZeroDivisionError: float floor division by zero
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__rdivmod__
documentation:
The pair `(other // NA, other % NA)` (`divmod(other, NA)`).

```pycon
>>> divmod(1024, NA)
(nan, nan)
>>> divmod(1024.0, NA)
(nan, nan)
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__pos__
documentation:
Return {data}`math.nan` (`+NA`).

nan
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__neg__
documentation:
Return {data}`math.nan` (`-NA`).

nan
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__abs__
documentation:
Return {data}`math.nan` (`abs(NA)`).

nan
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__round__
documentation:
Round {const}`nvitop.NA` to `ndigits` decimal places, defaulting to {const}`0`.

If `ndigits` is omitted or {data}`None`, returns {const}`0`, otherwise returns {data}`math.nan`.

```pycon
>>> round(NA)
0
>>> round(NA, 0)
nan
>>> round(NA, 1)
nan
```

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__lt__
documentation: The {const}`nvitop.NA` is always greater than any number, or uses the dictionary order for string.

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__le__
documentation: The {const}`nvitop.NA` is always greater than any number, or uses the dictionary order for string.

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__gt__
documentation: The {const}`nvitop.NA` is always greater than any number, or uses the dictionary order for string.

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__ge__
documentation: The {const}`nvitop.NA` is always greater than any number, or uses the dictionary order for string.

methods.name: nvitop.api.utils.NaType.__format__
documentation: Format {const}`nvitop.NA` according to `format_spec`.

A singleton ({const}`str: 'N/A'`) class represents a not applicable value.

The {const}`NA` instance behaves like a {class}`str` instance ({const}`'N/A'`) when doing string
manipulation (e.g. concatenation). For arithmetic operations, for example `NA / 1024 / 1024`,
it acts like the {data}`math.nan`.

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> NA
  'N/A'
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> 'memory usage: {}'.format(NA)  # NA is an instance of `str`
  'memory usage: N/A'
  >>> NA.lower()                     # NA is an instance of `str`
  'n/a'
  >>> NA.ljust(5)                    # NA is an instance of `str`
  'N/A  '
  >>> NA + ' str'                    # string contamination if the operand is a string
  'N/A str'
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> float(NA)                      # explicit conversion to float (`math.nan`)
  nan
  >>> NA + 1                         # auto-casting to float if the operand is a number
  nan
  >>> NA * 1024                      # auto-casting to float if the operand is a number
  nan
  >>> NA / (1024 * 1024)             # auto-casting to float if the operand is a number
  nan
  ```

methods.name: nvitop.api.process.HostProcess.oneshot
documentation:
A utility context manager which considerably speeds up the retrieval of multiple process information at the same time.

Internally different process info (e.g. name, ppid, uids, gids, ...) may be fetched by using
the same routine, but only one information is returned and the others are discarded. When
using this context manager the internal routine is executed once (in the example below on
`name()`) and the other info are cached.

The cache is cleared when exiting the context manager block. The advice is to use this every
time you retrieve more than one information about the process.

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> from nvitop import HostProcess
  >>> p = HostProcess()
  >>> with p.oneshot():
  ...     p.name()         # collect multiple info
  ...     p.cpu_times()    # return cached value
  ...     p.cpu_percent()  # return cached value
  ...     p.create_time()  # return cached value
  ```

methods.name: nvitop.api.process.HostProcess.as_snapshot
documentation: Return a onetime snapshot of the process.

Represent an OS process with the given PID.

If PID is omitted current process PID ({func}`os.getpid`) is used. The instance will be cache
during the lifetime of the process.

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> HostProcess()  # the current process
  HostProcess(pid=12345, name='python3', status='running', started='00:55:43')
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> p1 = HostProcess(12345)
  >>> p2 = HostProcess(12345)
  >>> p1 is p2                 # the same instance
  True
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> import copy
  >>> copy.deepcopy(p1) is p1  # the same instance
  True
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> p = HostProcess(pid=12345)
  >>> p.cmdline()
  ['python3', '-c', 'import IPython; IPython.terminal.ipapp.launch_new_instance()']
  >>> p.command()  # the result is in shell-escaped format
  'python3 -c "import IPython; IPython.terminal.ipapp.launch_new_instance()"'
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> p.as_snapshot()
  HostProcessSnapshot(
      real=HostProcess(pid=12345, name='python3', status='running', started='00:55:43'),
      cmdline=['python3', '-c', 'import IPython; IPython.terminal.ipapp.launch_new_instance()'],
      command='python3 -c "import IPython; IPython.terminal.ipapp.launch_new_instance()"',
      connections=[],
      cpu_percent=0.3,
      cpu_times=pcputimes(user=2.180019456, system=0.18424464, children_user=0.0, children_system=0.0),
      create_time=1656608143.31,
      cwd='/home/panxuehai',
      environ={...},
      ...
  )
  ```

methods.name: nvitop.api.process.GpuProcess.failsafe
documentation:
A context manager that enables fallback values for methods that fail.

Examples:
    >>> p = GpuProcess(pid=10000, device=Device(0))  # process does not exist
    >>> p
    GpuProcess(pid=10000, gpu_memory=N/A, type=N/A, device=PhysicalDevice(index=0, name="NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070", total_memory=8192MiB), host=HostProcess(pid=10000, status='terminated'))
    >>> p.cpu_percent()
    Traceback (most recent call last):
        ...
    NoSuchProcess: process no longer exists (pid=10000)

    >>> # Failsafe to the fallback value instead of raising exceptions
    ... with GpuProcess.failsafe():
    ...     print('fallback:              {!r}'.format(p.cpu_percent()))
    ...     print('fallback (float cast): {!r}'.format(float(p.cpu_percent())))  # `nvitop.NA` can be cast to float or int
    ...     print('fallback (int cast):   {!r}'.format(int(p.cpu_percent())))    # `nvitop.NA` can be cast to float or int
    fallback:              'N/A'
    fallback (float cast): nan
    fallback (int cast):   0

functions.name: nvitop.api.collector.take_snapshots
documentation:
Retrieve status of demanded devices and GPU processes.

Args:
: devices (Optional\[Union\[Device, Iterable[Device]\]\]):

  : Requested devices for snapshots. If not given, the devices will be determined from GPU
    processes: **(1)** All devices (no GPU processes are given); **(2)** Devices that used
    by given GPU processes.

  gpu_processes (Optional\[Union\[bool, GpuProcess, Iterable[GpuProcess]\]\]):

  : Requested GPU processes snapshots. If not given, all GPU processes running on the
    requested device will be returned. The GPU process snapshots can be suppressed by
    specifying `gpu_processes=False`.

Returns: SnapshotResult

: A named tuple containing two lists of snapshots.

Note:

: If not arguments are specified, all devices and all GPU processes will
  be returned.

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> from nvitop import take_snapshots, Device
  >>> import os
  >>> os.environ['CUDA_DEVICE_ORDER'] = 'PCI_BUS_ID'
  >>> os.environ['CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES'] = '1,0'
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> take_snapshots()  # equivalent to `take_snapshots(Device.all())`
  SnapshotResult(
      devices=[
          PhysicalDeviceSnapshot(
              real=PhysicalDevice(index=0, ...),
              ...
          ),
          ...
      ],
      gpu_processes=[
          GpuProcessSnapshot(
              real=GpuProcess(pid=xxxxxx, device=PhysicalDevice(index=0, ...), ...),
              ...
          ),
          ...
      ]
  )
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> device_snapshots, gpu_process_snapshots = take_snapshots(Device.all())  # type: Tuple[List[DeviceSnapshot], List[GpuProcessSnapshot]]
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> device_snapshots, _ = take_snapshots(gpu_processes=False)  # ignore process snapshots
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> take_snapshots(Device.cuda.all())  # use CUDA device enumeration
  SnapshotResult(
      devices=[
          CudaDeviceSnapshot(
              real=CudaDevice(cuda_index=0, physical_index=1, ...),
              ...
          ),
          CudaDeviceSnapshot(
              real=CudaDevice(cuda_index=1, physical_index=0, ...),
              ...
          ),
      ],
      gpu_processes=[
          GpuProcessSnapshot(
              real=GpuProcess(pid=xxxxxx, device=CudaDevice(cuda_index=0, ...), ...),
              ...
          ),
          ...
      ]
  )
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> take_snapshots(Device.cuda(1))  # <CUDA 1> only
  SnapshotResult(
      devices=[
          CudaDeviceSnapshot(
              real=CudaDevice(cuda_index=1, physical_index=0, ...),
              ...
          )
      ],
      gpu_processes=[
          GpuProcessSnapshot(
              real=GpuProcess(pid=xxxxxx, device=CudaDevice(cuda_index=1, ...), ...),
              ...
          ),
          ...
      ]
  )
  ```
pylint: disable-next=too-many-branches

functions.name: nvitop.api.collector.collect_in_background
documentation:
Start a background daemon thread that collect and call the callback function periodically.

See also {func}`ResourceMetricCollector.daemonize`.

Args:
: on_collect (Callable\[\[Dict[str, float]\], bool\]):

  : A callback function that will be called periodically. It takes a dictionary containing
    the resource metrics and returns a boolean indicating whether to continue monitoring.

  collector (Optional[ResourceMetricCollector]):

  : A {class}`ResourceMetricCollector` instance to collect metrics. If not given, it will
    collect metrics for all GPUs and subprocess of the current process.

  interval (Optional[float]):

  : The collect interval. If not given, use `collector.interval`.

  on_start (Optional\[Callable\[[ResourceMetricCollector], None\]\]):

  : A function to initialize the daemon thread and collector.

  on_stop (Optional\[Callable\[[ResourceMetricCollector], None\]\]):

  : A function that do some necessary cleanup after the daemon thread is stopped.

  tag (str):

  : The tag prefix used for metrics results.

  start (bool):

  : Whether to start the daemon thread on return.

Returns: threading.Thread

: A daemon thread object.

Examples:
: ```python
  logger = ...

  def on_collect(metrics):  # will be called periodically
      if logger.is_closed():  # closed manually by user
          return False
      logger.log(metrics)
      return True

  def on_stop(collector):  # will be called only once at stop
      if not logger.is_closed():
          logger.close()  # cleanup

  # Record metrics to the logger in the background every 5 seconds.
  # It will collect 5-second mean/min/max for each metric.
  collect_in_background(
      on_collect,
      ResourceMetricCollector(Device.cuda.all()),
      interval=5.0,
      on_stop=on_stop,
  )
  ```
pylint: disable-next=too-many-arguments

methods.name: nvitop.api.collector.ResourceMetricCollector.__init__

methods.name: nvitop.api.collector.ResourceMetricCollector.activate
documentation:
Start a new metric collection with the given tag.

Args:
    tag (str):
        The name of the new metric collection. The tag will be used to identify the metric
        collection. It must be a unique string.

Examples:
    >>> collector = ResourceMetricCollector()

    >>> collector.activate(tag='train')  # key prefix -> 'train'
    >>> collector.activate(tag='batch')  # key prefix -> 'train/batch'
    >>> collector.deactivate()           # key prefix -> 'train'
    >>> collector.deactivate()           # the collector has been stopped
    >>> collector.activate(tag='test')   # key prefix -> 'test'

methods.name: nvitop.api.collector.ResourceMetricCollector.deactivate
documentation:
Stop the current collection with the given tag and remove all sub-tags.

If the tag is not specified, deactivate the current active collection. For nested
collections, the sub-collections will be deactivated as well.

Args:
: tag (Optional[str]):

  : The tag to deactivate. If {data}`None`, the current active collection will be used.

methods.name: nvitop.api.collector.ResourceMetricCollector.context
documentation:
A context manager for starting and stopping resource metric collection.

Args:
    tag (str):
        The name of the new metric collection. The tag will be used to identify the metric
        collection. It must be a unique string.

Examples:
    >>> collector = ResourceMetricCollector()

    >>> with collector.context(tag='train'):  # key prefix -> 'train'
    ...     # Do something
    ...     collector.collect()  # -> Dict[str, float]

methods.name: nvitop.api.collector.ResourceMetricCollector.clear
documentation:
Reset the metric collection with the given tag.

If the tag is not specified, reset the current active collection. For nested collections,
the sub-collections will be reset as well.

Args:
: tag (Optional[str]):

  : The tag to reset. If {data}`None`, the current active collection will be reset.

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> collector = ResourceMetricCollector()
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> with collector(tag='train'):          # key prefix -> 'train'
  ...     time.sleep(5.0)
  ...     collector.collect()               # metrics within the 5.0s interval
  ...
  ...     time.sleep(5.0)
  ...     collector.collect()               # metrics within the cumulative 10.0s interval
  ...
  ...     collector.reset()                 # reset the active collection
  ...     time.sleep(5.0)
  ...     collector.collect()               # metrics within the 5.0s interval
  ...
  ...     with collector(tag='batch'):      # key prefix -> 'train/batch'
  ...         collector.reset(tag='train')  # reset both 'train' and 'train/batch'
  ```

methods.name: nvitop.api.collector.ResourceMetricCollector.daemonize
documentation:
Start a background daemon thread that collect and call the callback function periodically.

See also {func}`collect_in_background`.

Args:
: on_collect (Callable\[\[Dict[str, float]\], bool\]):

  : A callback function that will be called periodically. It takes a dictionary containing
    the resource metrics and returns a boolean indicating whether to continue monitoring.

  interval (Optional[float]):

  : The collect interval. If not given, use `collector.interval`.

  on_start (Optional\[Callable\[[ResourceMetricCollector], None\]\]):

  : A function to initialize the daemon thread and collector.

  on_stop (Optional\[Callable\[[ResourceMetricCollector], None\]\]):

  : A function that do some necessary cleanup after the daemon thread is stopped.

  tag (str):

  : The tag prefix used for metrics results.

  start (bool):

  : Whether to start the daemon thread on return.

Returns: threading.Thread

: A daemon thread object.

Examples:
: ```python
  logger = ...

  def on_collect(metrics):  # will be called periodically
      if logger.is_closed():  # closed manually by user
          return False
      logger.log(metrics)
      return True

  def on_stop(collector):  # will be called only once at stop
      if not logger.is_closed():
          logger.close()  # cleanup

  # Record metrics to the logger in the background every 5 seconds.
  # It will collect 5-second mean/min/max for each metric.
  ResourceMetricCollector(Device.cuda.all()).daemonize(
      on_collect,
      ResourceMetricCollector(Device.cuda.all()),
      interval=5.0,
      on_stop=on_stop,
  )
  ```
pylint: disable-next=too-many-arguments

class_variables.name: nvitop.api.collector.ResourceMetricCollector.__call__
documentation: alias for `with collector(tag='<tag>')`

A class for collecting resource metrics.

Args:
: devices (Iterable[Device]):

  : Set of Device instances for logging. If not given, all physical devices on board will be
    used.

  root_pids (Set[int]):

  : A set of PIDs, only the status of the descendant processes on the GPUs will be collected.
    If not given, the PID of the current process will be used.

  interval (float):

  : The snapshot interval for background daemon thread.

Core methods:

```python
collector.activate(tag='<tag>')  # alias: start
collector.deactivate()           # alias: stop
collector.reset(tag='<tag>')
collector.collect()

with collector(tag='<tag>'):
    ...

collector.daemonize(on_collect_fn)
```

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> import os
  >>> os.environ['CUDA_DEVICE_ORDER'] = 'PCI_BUS_ID'
  >>> os.environ['CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES'] = '3,2,1,0'
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> from nvitop import ResourceMetricCollector, Device
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> collector = ResourceMetricCollector()                           # log all devices and descendant processes of the current process on the GPUs
  >>> collector = ResourceMetricCollector(root_pids={1})              # log all devices and all GPU processes
  >>> collector = ResourceMetricCollector(devices=Device.cuda.all())  # use the CUDA ordinal
  ```

  >>> with collector(tag='<tag>'):

variables.name: nvitop.version.PYNVML_VERSION_CANDIDATES
documentation:
The list of supported `nvidia-ml-py` versions.
See also: [nvidia-ml-py's Release History](https://pypi.org/project/nvidia-ml-py/#history).

To install `nvitop` with a specific version of `nvidia-ml-py`, use `nvitop[pynvml-xx.yyy.zzz]`, for example:

```bash
pip3 install 'nvitop[pynvml-11.450.51]'
```

or

```bash
pip3 install nvitop nvidia-ml-py==11.450.51
```

Note:

: The package `nvidia-ml-py` is not backward compatible over releases. This may cause problems
  such as *"Function Not Found"* errors with old versions of NVIDIA drivers (e.g. the NVIDIA R430
  driver on Ubuntu 16.04 LTS).
  The ideal solution is to let the user install the best-fit version of `nvidia-ml-py`.
  See also: [nvidia-ml-py's Release History](https://pypi.org/project/nvidia-ml-py/#history).

  `nvidia-ml-py==11.450.51` is the last version supports the NVIDIA R430 driver (CUDA 10.x).
  Since `nvidia-ml-py>=11.450.129`, the definition of struct `nvmlProcessInfo_t` has introduced
  two new fields `gpuInstanceId` and `computeInstanceId` (GI ID and CI ID in newer `nvidia-smi`)
  which are incompatible with some old NVIDIA drivers. `nvitop` may not display the processes
  correctly due to this incompatibility.

functions.name: nvitop.api.libcuda._extract_cuda_errors_as_classes
documentation:
Generate a hierarchy of classes on top of {class}`CUDAError` class.

Each CUDA Error gets a new {class}`CUDAError` subclass. This way try-except blocks can filter
appropriate exceptions more easily.

{class}`CUDAError` is a parent class. Each `CUDA_ERROR_*` gets it's own subclass.
e.g. {data}`CUDA_ERROR_INVALID_VALUE` will be turned into {class}`CUDAError_InvalidValue`.

functions.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.cuInit
documentation:
Initialize the CUDA driver API.

Initialize the driver API and must be called before any other function from the driver API.
Currently, the `flags` parameter must be {data}`0`. If {func}`cuInit` has not been called,
any function from the driver API will return {data}`CUDA_ERROR_NOT_INITIALIZED`.

Raises:
: CUDAError_NoDevice:

  : If no CUDA-capable device is available.

  CUDAError_InvalidDevice:

  : If the device ordinal supplied by the user does not correspond to a valid CUDA device or
    that the action requested is invalid for the specified device.

  CUDAError_SystemDriverMismatch:

  : If there is a mismatch between the versions of the display driver and the CUDA driver.

  CUDAError_CompatNotSupportedOnDevice:

  : If the system was upgraded to run with forward compatibility but the visible hardware
    detected by CUDA does not support this configuration.

  CUDAError_InvalidValue:

  : If passed with invalid flag value.

  CUDAError_NotInitialized:

  : If cannot found the CUDA driver library.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_SUCCESS
documentation: The API call returned with no errors. In the case of query calls, this also means that the operation being queried is complete (see {func}`cuEventQuery` and {func}`cuStreamQuery`).

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_INVALID_VALUE
documentation: This indicates that one or more of the parameters passed to the API call is not within an acceptable range of values.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_OUT_OF_MEMORY
documentation: The API call failed because it was unable to allocate enough memory to perform the requested operation.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_NOT_INITIALIZED
documentation: This indicates that the CUDA driver has not been initialized with {func}`cuInit` or that initialization has failed.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_DEINITIALIZED
documentation: This indicates that the CUDA driver is in the process of shutting down.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_PROFILER_DISABLED
documentation: This indicates profiler is not initialized for this run. This can happen when the application is running with external profiling tools like visual profiler.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_STUB_LIBRARY
documentation: This indicates that the CUDA driver that the application has loaded is a stub library. Applications that run with the stub rather than a real driver loaded will result in CUDA API returning this error.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_DEVICE_UNAVAILABLE
documentation: This indicates that requested CUDA device is unavailable at the current time. Devices are often unavailable due to use of {data}`CU_COMPUTEMODE_EXCLUSIVE_PROCESS` or {data}`CU_COMPUTEMODE_PROHIBITED`.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_NO_DEVICE
documentation: This indicates that no CUDA - capable devices were detected by the installed CUDA driver.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_INVALID_DEVICE
documentation: This indicates that the device ordinal supplied by the user does not correspond to a valid CUDA device or that the action requested is invalid for the specified device.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_DEVICE_NOT_LICENSED
documentation: This error indicates that the Grid license is not applied.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_INVALID_IMAGE
documentation: This indicates that the device kernel image is invalid. This can also indicate an invalid CUDA module.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_INVALID_CONTEXT
documentation: This most frequently indicates that there is no context bound to the current thread. This can also be returned if the context passed to an API call is not a valid handle (such as a context that has had {func}`cuCtxDestroy` invoked on it). This can also be returned if a user mixes different API versions (i.e. 3010 context with 3020 API calls). See {func}`cuCtxGetApiVersion` for more details.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_MAP_FAILED
documentation: This indicates that a map or register operation has failed.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_UNMAP_FAILED
documentation: This indicates that an unmap or unregister operation has failed.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_ARRAY_IS_MAPPED
documentation: This indicates that the specified array is currently mapped and thus cannot be destroyed.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_ALREADY_MAPPED
documentation: This indicates that the resource is already mapped.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_NO_BINARY_FOR_GPU
documentation: This indicates that there is no kernel image available that is suitable for the device. This can occur when a user specifies code generation options for a particular CUDA source file that do not include the corresponding device configuration.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_ALREADY_ACQUIRED
documentation: This indicates that a resource has already been acquired.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_NOT_MAPPED
documentation: This indicates that a resource is not mapped.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_NOT_MAPPED_AS_ARRAY
documentation: This indicates that a mapped resource is not available for access as an array.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_NOT_MAPPED_AS_POINTER
documentation: This indicates that a mapped resource is not available for access as a pointer.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_ECC_UNCORRECTABLE
documentation: This indicates that an uncorrectable ECC error was detected during execution.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_UNSUPPORTED_LIMIT
documentation: This indicates that the {class}`CUlimit` passed to the API call is not supported by the active device.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_CONTEXT_ALREADY_IN_USE
documentation: This indicates that the {class}`CUcontext` passed to the API call can only be bound to a single CPU thread at a time but is already bound to a CPU thread.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_PEER_ACCESS_UNSUPPORTED
documentation: This indicates that peer access is not supported across the given devices.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_INVALID_PTX
documentation: This indicates that a PTX JIT compilation failed.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_INVALID_GRAPHICS_CONTEXT
documentation: This indicates an error with OpenGL or DirectX context.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_NVLINK_UNCORRECTABLE
documentation: This indicates that an uncorrectable NVLink error was detected during the execution.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_JIT_COMPILER_NOT_FOUND
documentation: This indicates that the PTX JIT compiler library was not found.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_UNSUPPORTED_PTX_VERSION
documentation: This indicates that the provided PTX was compiled with an unsupported toolchain.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_JIT_COMPILATION_DISABLED
documentation: This indicates that the PTX JIT compilation was disabled.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_UNSUPPORTED_EXEC_AFFINITY
documentation: This indicates that the {class}`CUexecAffinityType` passed to the API call is not supported by the active device.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_INVALID_SOURCE
documentation: This indicates that the device kernel source is invalid. This includes compilation / linker errors encountered in device code or user error.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_FILE_NOT_FOUND
documentation: This indicates that the file specified was not found.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_SHARED_OBJECT_SYMBOL_NOT_FOUND
documentation: This indicates that a link to a shared object failed to resolve.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_SHARED_OBJECT_INIT_FAILED
documentation: This indicates that initialization of a shared object failed.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_OPERATING_SYSTEM
documentation: This indicates that an OS call failed.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_INVALID_HANDLE
documentation: This indicates that a resource handle passed to the API call was not valid. Resource handles are opaque types like {class}`CUstream` and {class}`CUevent`.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_ILLEGAL_STATE
documentation: This indicates that a resource required by the API call is not in a valid state to perform the requested operation.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_NOT_FOUND
documentation: This indicates that a named symbol was not found. Examples of symbols are global / constant variable names, driver function names, texture names, and surface names.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_NOT_READY
documentation: This indicates that asynchronous operations issued previously have not completed yet. This result is not actually an error, but must be indicated differently than {data}`CUDA_SUCCESS` (which indicates completion). Calls that may return this value include {func}`cuEventQuery` and {func}`cuStreamQuery`.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_ILLEGAL_ADDRESS
documentation: While executing a kernel, the device encountered a load or store instruction on an invalid memory address. This leaves the process in an inconsistent state and any further CUDA work will return the same error. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_LAUNCH_OUT_OF_RESOURCES
documentation: This indicates that a launch did not occur because it did not have appropriate resources. This error usually indicates that the user has attempted to pass too many arguments to the device kernel, or the kernel launch specifies too many threads for the kernel's register count. Passing arguments of the wrong size (i.e. a 64 - bit pointer when a 32 - bit int is expected) is equivalent to passing too many arguments and can also result in this error.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_LAUNCH_TIMEOUT
documentation: This indicates that the device kernel took too long to execute. This can only occur if timeouts are enabled - see the device attribute {data}`CU_DEVICE_ATTRIBUTE_KERNEL_EXEC_TIMEOUT` for more information. This leaves the process in an inconsistent state and any further CUDA work will return the same error. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_LAUNCH_INCOMPATIBLE_TEXTURING
documentation: This error indicates a kernel launch that uses an incompatible texturing mode.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_PEER_ACCESS_ALREADY_ENABLED
documentation: This error indicates that a call to {func}`cuCtxEnablePeerAccess` is trying to re - enable peer access to a context which has already had peer access to it enabled.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_PEER_ACCESS_NOT_ENABLED
documentation: This error indicates that {func}`cuCtxDisablePeerAccess` is trying to disable peer access which has not been enabled yet via {func}`cuCtxEnablePeerAccess`.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_PRIMARY_CONTEXT_ACTIVE
documentation: This error indicates that the primary context for the specified device has already been initialized.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_CONTEXT_IS_DESTROYED
documentation: This error indicates that the context current to the calling thread has been destroyed using {func}`cuCtxDestroy`, or is a primary context which has not yet been initialized.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_ASSERT
documentation: A device - side assert triggered during kernel execution. The context cannot be used anymore, and must be destroyed. All existing device memory allocations from this context are invalid and must be reconstructed if the program is to continue using CUDA.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_TOO_MANY_PEERS
documentation: This error indicates that the hardware resources required to enable peer access have been exhausted for one or more of the devices passed to {func}`cuCtxEnablePeerAccess`.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_HOST_MEMORY_ALREADY_REGISTERED
documentation: This error indicates that the memory range passed to {func}`cuMemHostRegister` has already been registered.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_HOST_MEMORY_NOT_REGISTERED
documentation: This error indicates that the pointer passed to {func}`cuMemHostUnregister` does not correspond to any currently registered memory region.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_HARDWARE_STACK_ERROR
documentation: While executing a kernel, the device encountered a stack error. This can be due to stack corruption or exceeding the stack size limit. This leaves the process in an inconsistent state and any further CUDA work will return the same error. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_ILLEGAL_INSTRUCTION
documentation: While executing a kernel, the device encountered an illegal instruction. This leaves the process in an inconsistent state and any further CUDA work will return the same error. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_MISALIGNED_ADDRESS
documentation: While executing a kernel, the device encountered a load or store instruction on a memory address which is not aligned. This leaves the process in an inconsistent state and any further CUDA work will return the same error. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_INVALID_ADDRESS_SPACE
documentation: While executing a kernel, the device encountered an instruction which can only operate on memory locations in certain address spaces (global, shared, or local), but was supplied a memory address not belonging to an allowed address space. This leaves the process in an inconsistent state and any further CUDA work will return the same error. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_INVALID_PC
documentation: While executing a kernel, the device program counter wrapped its address space. This leaves the process in an inconsistent state and any further CUDA work will return the same error. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_LAUNCH_FAILED
documentation: An exception occurred on the device while executing a kernel. Common causes include dereferencing an invalid device pointer and accessing out of bounds shared memory. Less common cases can be system specific - more information about these cases can be found in the system specific user guide. This leaves the process in an inconsistent state and any further CUDA work will return the same error. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_COOPERATIVE_LAUNCH_TOO_LARGE
documentation: This error indicates that the number of blocks launched per grid for a kernel that was launched via either {func}`cuLaunchCooperativeKernel` or {func}`cuLaunchCooperativeKernelMultiDevice` exceeds the maximum number of blocks as allowed by {func}`cuOccupancyMaxActiveBlocksPerMultiprocessor` or {func}`cuOccupancyMaxActiveBlocksPerMultiprocessorWithFlags` times the number of multiprocessors as specified by the device attribute {data}`CU_DEVICE_ATTRIBUTE_MULTIPROCESSOR_COUNT`.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_NOT_PERMITTED
documentation: This error indicates that the attempted operation is not permitted.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED
documentation: This error indicates that the attempted operation is not supported on the current system or device.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_SYSTEM_NOT_READY
documentation: This error indicates that the system is not yet ready to start any CUDA work. To continue using CUDA, verify the system configuration is in a valid state and all required driver daemons are actively running. More information about this error can be found in the system specific user guide.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_SYSTEM_DRIVER_MISMATCH
documentation: This error indicates that there is a mismatch between the versions of the display driver and the CUDA driver. Refer to the compatibility documentation for supported versions.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_COMPAT_NOT_SUPPORTED_ON_DEVICE
documentation: This error indicates that the system was upgraded to run with forward compatibility but the visible hardware detected by CUDA does not support this configuration. Refer to the compatibility documentation for the supported hardware matrix or ensure that only supported hardware is visible during initialization via the `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES` environment variable.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_MPS_CONNECTION_FAILED
documentation: This error indicates that the MPS client failed to connect to the MPS control daemon or the MPS server.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_MPS_RPC_FAILURE
documentation: This error indicates that the remote procedural call between the MPS server and the MPS client failed.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_MPS_SERVER_NOT_READY
documentation: This error indicates that the MPS server is not ready to accept new MPS client requests. This error can be returned when the MPS server is in the process of recovering from a fatal failure.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_MPS_MAX_CLIENTS_REACHED
documentation: This error indicates that the hardware resources required to create MPS client have been exhausted.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_MPS_MAX_CONNECTIONS_REACHED
documentation: This error indicates the the hardware resources required to support device connections have been exhausted.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_STREAM_CAPTURE_UNSUPPORTED
documentation: This error indicates that the operation is not permitted when the stream is capturing.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_STREAM_CAPTURE_INVALIDATED
documentation: This error indicates that the current capture sequence on the stream has been invalidated due to a previous error.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_STREAM_CAPTURE_MERGE
documentation: This error indicates that the operation would have resulted in a merge of two independent capture sequences.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_STREAM_CAPTURE_UNMATCHED
documentation: This error indicates that the capture was initiated not in this stream.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_STREAM_CAPTURE_UNJOINED
documentation: This error indicates that the capture sequence contains a fork that was not joined to the primary stream.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_STREAM_CAPTURE_ISOLATION
documentation: This error indicates that a dependency would have been created which crosses the capture sequence boundary. Only implicit in -stream ordering dependencies are allowed to cross the boundary.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_STREAM_CAPTURE_IMPLICIT
documentation: This error indicates a disallowed implicit dependency on a current capture sequence from {func}`cudaStreamLegacy`.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_CAPTURED_EVENT
documentation: This error indicates that the operation is not permitted on an event which was last recorded in a capturing stream.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_STREAM_CAPTURE_WRONG_THREAD
documentation: A stream capture sequence not initiated with the {data}`CU_STREAM_CAPTURE_MODE_RELAXED` argument to {func}`cuStreamBeginCapture` was passed to {func}`cuStreamEndCapture` in a different thread.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_TIMEOUT
documentation: This error indicates that the timeout specified for the wait operation has lapsed.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_FAILURE
documentation: This error indicates that the graph update was not performed because it included changes which violated constraints specific to instantiated graph update.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_EXTERNAL_DEVICE
documentation: This indicates that an async error has occurred in a device outside of CUDA. If CUDA was waiting for an external device's signal before consuming shared data, the external device signaled an error indicating that the data is not valid for consumption. This leaves the process in an inconsistent state and any further CUDA work will return the same error. To continue using CUDA, the process must be terminated and relaunched.

variables.name: nvitop.api.libcuda.CUDA_ERROR_UNKNOWN
documentation: This indicates that an unknown internal error has occurred.
